<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
import os
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL_SYNC', 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx')
try:
    engine = create_engine(url)
    with engine.connect() as conn:
        r = conn.execute(text('SELECT 1'))
        print('Database connection: OK')
        r = conn.execute(text(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public' ORDER BY tablename\"))
        tables = [row[0] for row in r]
        print('Tables:', tables)
        if 'auth_users' in tables:
            r2 = conn.execute(text('SELECT id, email, role FROM auth_users'))
            users = list(r2)
            print('Auth users:', users)
except Exception as e:
    print('Error:', e)
" 2>&1</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
import os
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = os.environ.get('DATABASE_URL_SYNC', 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx')
try:
    engine = create_engine(url)
    with engine.connect() as conn:
        r = conn.execute(text('SELECT 1'))
        print('Database connection: OK')
        r = conn.execute(text(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public' ORDER BY tablename\"))
        tables = [row[0] for row in r]
        print('Tables:', tables)
        if 'auth_users' in tables:
            r2 = conn.execute(text('SELECT id, email, role FROM auth_users'))
            users = list(r2)
            print('Auth users:', users)
except Exception as e:
    print('Error:', e)
" 2>&1
```

```
Database connection: OK
Tables: ['alembic_version', 'alerts', 'auth_users', 'cameras', 'detections', 'objects', 'unknown_faces', 'users']
Auth users: [(1, 'admin@visioryx.dev', 'admin')]

```
</details>
</tool-use>

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx'
engine = create_engine(url)
with engine.connect() as conn:
    for t in ['cameras', 'users', 'detections', 'alerts']:
        r = conn.execute(text(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {t}'))
        print(f'{t}: {r.scalar()} rows')
" 2>&1</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx'
engine = create_engine(url)
with engine.connect() as conn:
    for t in ['cameras', 'users', 'detections', 'alerts']:
        r = conn.execute(text(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {t}'))
        print(f'{t}: {r.scalar()} rows')
" 2>&1
```

```
users: 2 rows
detections: 3 rows
alerts: 3 rows
```

Result:

```json
{
  "url": "https://pypi.org/pypi/fastapi/json",
  "markdown": "{\"info\":{\"author\":null,\"author_email\":\"Sebastián Ramírez \",\"bugtrack_url\":null,\"classifiers\":[\"Development Status :: 4 - Beta\",\"Environment :: Web Environment\",\"Framework :: AsyncIO\",\"Framework :: FastAPI\",\"Framework :: Pydantic\",\"Framework :: Pydantic :: 2\",\"Intended Audience :: Developers\",\"Intended Audience :: Information Technology\",\"Intended Audience :: System Administrators\",\"Operating System :: OS Independent\",\"Programming Language :: Python\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3 :: Only\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13\",\"Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14\",\"Topic :: Internet\",\"Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP\",\"Topic :: Internet :: WWW/HTTP :: HTTP Servers\",\"Topic :: Software Development\",\"Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries\",\"Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Application Frameworks\",\"Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules\",\"Typing :: Typed\"],\"description\":\" \\n \\n \\n \\n FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n\\n---\\n\\n**Documentation**: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com \\n\\n**Source Code**: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi \\n\\n---\\n\\nFastAPI is a modern, fast (high-performance), web framework for building APIs with Python based on standard Python type hints.\\n\\nThe key features are:\\n\\n* **Fast**: Very high performance, on par with **NodeJS** and **Go** (thanks to Starlette and Pydantic). [One of the fastest Python frameworks available](#performance).\\n* **Fast to code**: Increase the speed to develop features by about 200% to 300%. *\\n* **Fewer bugs**: Reduce about 40% of human (developer) induced errors. *\\n* **Intuitive**: Great editor support. Completion everywhere. Less time debugging.\\n* **Easy**: Designed to be easy to use and learn. Less time reading docs.\\n* **Short**: Minimize code duplication. Multiple features from each parameter declaration. Fewer bugs.\\n* **Robust**: Get production-ready code. With automatic interactive documentation.\\n* **Standards-based**: Based on (and fully compatible with) the open standards for APIs: OpenAPI (previously known as Swagger) and JSON Schema.\\n\\n * estimation based on tests conducted by an internal development team, building production applications. \\n\\n## Sponsors\\n\\n \\n### Keystone Sponsor\\n\\n \\n\\n### Gold and Silver Sponsors\\n\\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n \\n\\n \\n\\n Other sponsors \\n\\n## Opinions\\n\\n\\\"_[...] I'm using **FastAPI** a ton these days. [...] I'm actually planning to use it for all of my team's **ML services at Microsoft**. Some of them are getting integrated into the core **Windows** product and some **Office** products._\\\"\\n\\n Kabir Khan - Microsoft (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_We adopted the **FastAPI** library to spawn a **REST** server that can be queried to obtain **predictions**. [for Ludwig]_\\\"\\n\\n Piero Molino, Yaroslav Dudin, and Sai Sumanth Miryala - Uber (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_**Netflix** is pleased to announce the open-source release of our **crisis management** orchestration framework: **Dispatch**! [built with **FastAPI**]_\\\"\\n\\n Kevin Glisson, Marc Vilanova, Forest Monsen - Netflix (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_I’m over the moon excited about **FastAPI**. It’s so fun!_\\\"\\n\\n Brian Okken - Python Bytes podcast host (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_Honestly, what you've built looks super solid and polished. In many ways, it's what I wanted **Hug** to be - it's really inspiring to see someone build that._\\\"\\n\\n Timothy Crosley - Hug creator (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_If you're looking to learn one **modern framework** for building REST APIs, check out **FastAPI** [...] It's fast, easy to use and easy to learn [...]_\\\"\\n\\n\\\"_We've switched over to **FastAPI** for our **APIs** [...] I think you'll like it [...]_\\\"\\n\\n Ines Montani - Matthew Honnibal - Explosion AI founders - spaCy creators (ref) - (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n\\\"_If anyone is looking to build a production Python API, I would highly recommend **FastAPI**. It is **beautifully designed**, **simple to use** and **highly scalable**, it has become a **key component** in our API first development strategy and is driving many automations and services such as our Virtual TAC Engineer._\\\"\\n\\n Deon Pillsbury - Cisco (ref) \\n\\n---\\n\\n## FastAPI mini documentary\\n\\nThere's a FastAPI mini documentary released at the end of 2025, you can watch it online:\\n\\n \\n\\n## **Typer**, the FastAPI of CLIs\\n\\n \\n\\nIf you are building a CLI app to be used in the terminal instead of a web API, check out **Typer**.\\n\\n**Typer** is FastAPI's little sibling. And it's intended to be the **FastAPI of CLIs**. ⌨️ 🚀\\n\\n## Requirements\\n\\nFastAPI stands on the shoulders of giants:\\n\\n* Starlette for the web parts.\\n* Pydantic for the data parts.\\n\\n## Installation\\n\\nCreate and activate a virtual environment and then install FastAPI:\\n\\n \\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;console\\n$ pip install \\\"fastapi[standard]\\\"\\n\\n---&gt; 100%\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n \\n\\n**Note**: Make sure you put &#96;\\\"fastapi[standard]\\\"&#96; in quotes to ensure it works in all terminals.\\n\\n## Example\\n\\n### Create it\\n\\nCreate a file &#96;main.py&#96; with:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\n\\napp = FastAPI()\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/\\\")\\ndef read_root():\\n return {\\\"Hello\\\": \\\"World\\\"}\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/items/{item_id}\\\")\\ndef read_item(item_id: int, q: str | None = None):\\n return {\\\"item_id\\\": item_id, \\\"q\\\": q}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n \\n Or use async def... \\n\\nIf your code uses &#96;async&#96; / &#96;await&#96;, use &#96;async def&#96;:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python hl_lines=\\\"7 12\\\"\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\n\\napp = FastAPI()\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/\\\")\\nasync def read_root():\\n return {\\\"Hello\\\": \\\"World\\\"}\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/items/{item_id}\\\")\\nasync def read_item(item_id: int, q: str | None = None):\\n return {\\\"item_id\\\": item_id, \\\"q\\\": q}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n**Note**:\\n\\nIf you don't know, check the _\\\"In a hurry?\\\"_ section about &#96;async&#96; and &#96;await&#96; in the docs.\\n\\n \\n\\n### Run it\\n\\nRun the server with:\\n\\n \\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;console\\n$ fastapi dev main.py\\n\\n ╭────────── FastAPI CLI - Development mode ───────────╮\\n │ │\\n │ Serving at: http://127.0.0.1:8000 │\\n │ │\\n │ API docs: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs │\\n │ │\\n │ Running in development mode, for production use: │\\n │ │\\n │ fastapi run │\\n │ │\\n ╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯\\n\\nINFO: Will watch for changes in these directories: ['/home/user/code/awesomeapp']\\nINFO: Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit)\\nINFO: Started reloader process [2248755] using WatchFiles\\nINFO: Started server process [2248757]\\nINFO: Waiting for application startup.\\nINFO: Application startup complete.\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n \\n\\n \\n About the command fastapi dev main.py... \\n\\nThe command &#96;fastapi dev&#96; reads your &#96;main.py&#96; file, detects the **FastAPI** app in it, and starts a server using Uvicorn.\\n\\nBy default, &#96;fastapi dev&#96; will start with auto-reload enabled for local development.\\n\\nYou can read more about it in the FastAPI CLI docs.\\n\\n \\n\\n### Check it\\n\\nOpen your browser at http://127.0.0.1:8000/items/5?q=somequery.\\n\\nYou will see the JSON response as:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;JSON\\n{\\\"item_id\\\": 5, \\\"q\\\": \\\"somequery\\\"}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\nYou already created an API that:\\n\\n* Receives HTTP requests in the _paths_ &#96;/&#96; and &#96;/items/{item_id}&#96;.\\n* Both _paths_ take &#96;GET&#96; operations (also known as HTTP _methods_).\\n* The _path_ &#96;/items/{item_id}&#96; has a _path parameter_ &#96;item_id&#96; that should be an &#96;int&#96;.\\n* The _path_ &#96;/items/{item_id}&#96; has an optional &#96;str&#96; _query parameter_ &#96;q&#96;.\\n\\n### Interactive API docs\\n\\nNow go to http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs.\\n\\nYou will see the automatic interactive API documentation (provided by Swagger UI):\\n\\n![Swagger UI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-01-swagger-ui-simple.png)\\n\\n### Alternative API docs\\n\\nAnd now, go to http://127.0.0.1:8000/redoc.\\n\\nYou will see the alternative automatic documentation (provided by ReDoc):\\n\\n![ReDoc](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-02-redoc-simple.png)\\n\\n## Example upgrade\\n\\nNow modify the file &#96;main.py&#96; to receive a body from a &#96;PUT&#96; request.\\n\\nDeclare the body using standard Python types, thanks to Pydantic.\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python hl_lines=\\\"2 7-10 23-25\\\"\\nfrom fastapi import FastAPI\\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel\\n\\napp = FastAPI()\\n\\n\\nclass Item(BaseModel):\\n name: str\\n price: float\\n is_offer: bool | None = None\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/\\\")\\ndef read_root():\\n return {\\\"Hello\\\": \\\"World\\\"}\\n\\n\\n@app.get(\\\"/items/{item_id}\\\")\\ndef read_item(item_id: int, q: str | None = None):\\n return {\\\"item_id\\\": item_id, \\\"q\\\": q}\\n\\n\\n@app.put(\\\"/items/{item_id}\\\")\\ndef update_item(item_id: int, item: Item):\\n return {\\\"item_name\\\": item.name, \\\"item_id\\\": item_id}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\nThe &#96;fastapi dev&#96; server should reload automatically.\\n\\n### Interactive API docs upgrade\\n\\nNow go to http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs.\\n\\n* The interactive API documentation will be automatically updated, including the new body:\\n\\n![Swagger UI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-03-swagger-02.png)\\n\\n* Click on the button \\\"Try it out\\\", it allows you to fill the parameters and directly interact with the API:\\n\\n![Swagger UI interaction](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-04-swagger-03.png)\\n\\n* Then click on the \\\"Execute\\\" button, the user interface will communicate with your API, send the parameters, get the results and show them on the screen:\\n\\n![Swagger UI interaction](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-05-swagger-04.png)\\n\\n### Alternative API docs upgrade\\n\\nAnd now, go to http://127.0.0.1:8000/redoc.\\n\\n* The alternative documentation will also reflect the new query parameter and body:\\n\\n![ReDoc](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/index/index-06-redoc-02.png)\\n\\n### Recap\\n\\nIn summary, you declare **once** the types of parameters, body, etc. as function parameters.\\n\\nYou do that with standard modern Python types.\\n\\nYou don't have to learn a new syntax, the methods or classes of a specific library, etc.\\n\\nJust standard **Python**.\\n\\nFor example, for an &#96;int&#96;:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\nitem_id: int\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\nor for a more complex &#96;Item&#96; model:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\nitem: Item\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n...and with that single declaration you get:\\n\\n* Editor support, including:\\n * Completion.\\n * Type checks.\\n* Validation of data:\\n * Automatic and clear errors when the data is invalid.\\n * Validation even for deeply nested JSON objects.\\n* Conversion of input data: coming from the network to Python data and types. Reading from:\\n * JSON.\\n * Path parameters.\\n * Query parameters.\\n * Cookies.\\n * Headers.\\n * Forms.\\n * Files.\\n* Conversion of output data: converting from Python data and types to network data (as JSON):\\n * Convert Python types (&#96;str&#96;, &#96;int&#96;, &#96;float&#96;, &#96;bool&#96;, &#96;list&#96;, etc).\\n * &#96;datetime&#96; objects.\\n * &#96;UUID&#96; objects.\\n * Database models.\\n * ...and many more.\\n* Automatic interactive API documentation, including 2 alternative user interfaces:\\n * Swagger UI.\\n * ReDoc.\\n\\n---\\n\\nComing back to the previous code example, **FastAPI** will:\\n\\n* Validate that there is an &#96;item_id&#96; in the path for &#96;GET&#96; and &#96;PUT&#96; requests.\\n* Validate that the &#96;item_id&#96; is of type &#96;int&#96; for &#96;GET&#96; and &#96;PUT&#96; requests.\\n * If it is not, the client will see a useful, clear error.\\n* Check if there is an optional query parameter named &#96;q&#96; (as in &#96;http://127.0.0.1:8000/items/foo?q=somequery&#96;) for &#96;GET&#96; requests.\\n * As the &#96;q&#96; parameter is declared with &#96;= None&#96;, it is optional.\\n * Without the &#96;None&#96; it would be required (as is the body in the case with &#96;PUT&#96;).\\n* For &#96;PUT&#96; requests to &#96;/items/{item_id}&#96;, read the body as JSON:\\n * Check that it has a required attribute &#96;name&#96; that should be a &#96;str&#96;.\\n * Check that it has a required attribute &#96;price&#96; that has to be a &#96;float&#96;.\\n * Check that it has an optional attribute &#96;is_offer&#96;, that should be a &#96;bool&#96;, if present.\\n * All this would also work for deeply nested JSON objects.\\n* Convert from and to JSON automatically.\\n* Document everything with OpenAPI, that can be used by:\\n * Interactive documentation systems.\\n * Automatic client code generation systems, for many languages.\\n* Provide 2 interactive documentation web interfaces directly.\\n\\n---\\n\\nWe just scratched the surface, but you already get the idea of how it all works.\\n\\nTry changing the line with:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\n return {\\\"item_name\\\": item.name, \\\"item_id\\\": item_id}\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n...from:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\n ... \\\"item_name\\\": item.name ...\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n...to:\\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;Python\\n ... \\\"item_price\\\": item.price ...\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n...and see how your editor will auto-complete the attributes and know their types:\\n\\n![editor support](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/vscode-completion.png)\\n\\nFor a more complete example including more features, see the Tutorial - User Guide.\\n\\n**Spoiler alert**: the tutorial - user guide includes:\\n\\n* Declaration of **parameters** from other different places as: **headers**, **cookies**, **form fields** and **files**.\\n* How to set **validation constraints** as &#96;maximum_length&#96; or &#96;regex&#96;.\\n* A very powerful and easy to use ** Dependency Injection ** system.\\n* Security and authentication, including support for **OAuth2** with **JWT tokens** and **HTTP Basic** auth.\\n* More advanced (but equally easy) techniques for declaring **deeply nested JSON models** (thanks to Pydantic).\\n* **GraphQL** integration with Strawberry and other libraries.\\n* Many extra features (thanks to Starlette) as:\\n * **WebSockets**\\n * extremely easy tests based on HTTPX and &#96;pytest&#96;\\n * **CORS**\\n * **Cookie Sessions**\\n * ...and more.\\n\\n### Deploy your app (optional)\\n\\nYou can optionally deploy your FastAPI app to FastAPI Cloud, go and join the waiting list if you haven't. 🚀\\n\\nIf you already have a **FastAPI Cloud** account (we invited you from the waiting list 😉), you can deploy your application with one command.\\n\\nBefore deploying, make sure you are logged in:\\n\\n \\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;console\\n$ fastapi login\\n\\nYou are logged in to FastAPI Cloud 🚀\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n \\n\\nThen deploy your app:\\n\\n \\n\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;console\\n$ fastapi deploy\\n\\nDeploying to FastAPI Cloud...\\n\\n✅ Deployment successful!\\n\\n🐔 Ready the chicken! Your app is ready at https://myapp.fastapicloud.dev\\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\\n\\n \\n\\nThat's it! Now you can access your app at that URL. ✨\\n\\n#### About FastAPI Cloud\\n\\n** FastAPI Cloud ** is built by the same author and team behind **FastAPI**.\\n\\nIt streamlines the process of **building**, **deploying**, and **accessing** an API with minimal effort.\\n\\nIt brings the same **developer experience** of building apps with FastAPI to **deploying** them to the cloud. 🎉\\n\\nFastAPI Cloud is the primary sponsor and funding provider for the *FastAPI and friends* open source projects. ✨\\n\\n#### Deploy to other cloud providers\\n\\nFastAPI is open source and based on standards. You can deploy FastAPI apps to any cloud provider you choose.\\n\\nFollow your cloud provider's guides to deploy FastAPI apps with them. 🤓\\n\\n## Performance\\n\\nIndependent TechEmpower benchmarks show **FastAPI** applications running under Uvicorn as one of the fastest Python frameworks available, only below Starlette and Uvicorn themselves (used internally by FastAPI). (*)\\n\\nTo understand more about it, see the section Benchmarks.\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\nFastAPI depends on Pydantic and Starlette.\\n\\n### &#96;standard&#96; Dependencies\\n\\nWhen you install FastAPI with &#96;pip install \\\"fastapi[standard]\\\"&#96; it comes with the &#96;standard&#96; group of optional dependencies:\\n\\nUsed by Pydantic:\\n\\n* email-validator - for email validation.\\n\\nUsed by Starlette:\\n\\n* httpx - Required if you want to use the &#96;TestClient&#96;.\\n* jinja2 - Required if you want to use the default template configuration.\\n* python-multipart - Required if you want to support form \\\"parsing\\\", with &#96;request.form()&#96;.\\n\\nUsed by FastAPI:\\n\\n* uvicorn - for the server that loads and serves your application. This includes &#96;uvicorn[standard]&#96;, which includes some dependencies (e.g. &#96;uvloop&#96;) needed for high performance serving.\\n* &#96;fastapi-cli[standard]&#96; - to provide the &#96;fastapi&#96; command.\\n * This includes &#96;fastapi-cloud-cli&#96;, which allows you to deploy your FastAPI application to FastAPI Cloud.\\n\\n### Without &#96;standard&#96; Dependencies\\n\\nIf you don't want to include the &#96;standard&#96; optional dependencies, you can install with &#96;pip install fastapi&#96; instead of &#96;pip install \\\"fastapi[standard]\\\"&#96;.\\n\\n### Without &#96;fastapi-cloud-cli&#96;\\n\\nIf you want to install FastAPI with the standard dependencies but without the &#96;fastapi-cloud-cli&#96;, you can install with &#96;pip install \\\"fastapi[standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli]\\\"&#96;.\\n\\n### Additional Optional Dependencies\\n\\nThere are some additional dependencies you might want to install.\\n\\nAdditional optional Pydantic dependencies:\\n\\n* pydantic-settings - for settings management.\\n* pydantic-extra-types - for extra types to be used with Pydantic.\\n\\nAdditional optional FastAPI dependencies:\\n\\n* orjson - Required if you want to use &#96;ORJSONResponse&#96;.\\n* ujson - Required if you want to use &#96;UJSONResponse&#96;.\\n\\n## License\\n\\nThis project is licensed under the terms of the MIT license.\\n\",\"description_content_type\":\"text/markdown\",\"docs_url\":null,\"download_url\":null,\"downloads\":{\"last_day\":-1,\"last_month\":-1,\"last_week\":-1},\"dynamic\":null,\"home_page\":null,\"keywords\":null,\"license\":null,\"license_expression\":\"MIT\",\"license_files\":[\"LICENSE\"],\"maintainer\":null,\"maintainer_email\":null,\"name\":\"fastapi\",\"package_url\":\"https://pypi.org/project/fastapi/\",\"platform\":null,\"project_url\":\"https://pypi.org/project/fastapi/\",\"project_urls\":{\"Changelog\":\"https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/release-notes/\",\"Documentation\":\"https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/\",\"Homepage\":\"https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi\",\"Issues\":\"https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues\",\"Repository\":\"https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi\"},\"provides_extra\":[\"standard\",\"standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli\",\"all\"],\"release_url\":\"https://pypi.org/project/fastapi/0.135.1/\",\"requires_dist\":[\"starlette&gt;=0.46.0\",\"pydantic&gt;=2.7.0\",\"typing-extensions&gt;=4.8.0\",\"typing-inspection&gt;=0.4.2\",\"annotated-doc&gt;=0.0.2\",\"fastapi-cli[standard]&gt;=0.0.8; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"httpx&lt;1.0.0,&gt;=0.23.0; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"jinja2&gt;=3.1.5; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"python-multipart&gt;=0.0.18; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"email-validator&gt;=2.0.0; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"uvicorn[standard]&gt;=0.12.0; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"pydantic-settings&gt;=2.0.0; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"pydantic-extra-types&gt;=2.0.0; extra == \\\"standard\\\"\",\"fastapi-cli[standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli]&gt;=0.0.8; extra == \\\"standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli\\\"\",\"httpx&lt;1.0.0,&gt;=0.23.0; extra == \\\"standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli\\\"\",\"jinja2&gt;=3.1.5; extra == \\\"standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli\\\"\",\"python-multipart&gt;=0.0.18; extra == \\\"standard-no-fastapi-cloud-cli\\\"\",\"email-validator&gt;=2.0.0; extra == <details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
```
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx'
engine = create_engine(url)
with engine.connect() as conn:
    r = conn.execute(text(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public' ORDER BY tablename\"))
    tables = [row[0] for row in r]
    print('Tables:', list(tables))
" 2>&1</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/devendervutukuru/Desktop/My\ Projects/Visioryx/backend && PYTHONPATH=. python3 -c "
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
url = 'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx'
engine = create_engine(url)
with engine.connect() as conn:
    r = conn.execute(text(\"SELECT tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public' ORDER BY tablename\"))
    tables = [row[0] for row in r]
    print('Tables:', list(tables))
" 2>&1
```

Summary of what was checked and updated:

## Verification results

### 1. **Database**
- PostgreSQL is reachable at `localhost:5432`
- Tables present: `alembic_version`, `alerts`, `auth_users`, `cameras`, `detections`, `objects`, `unknown_faces`, `users`
- Data: 1 admin user, 4 cameras, 2 users, 3 detections, 3 alerts

### 2. **Backend API**
- Health: `http://localhost:8000/health` → `{"status":"healthy"}`
- DB health: `http://localhost:8000/health/db` → `{"status":"healthy","database":"connected"}`

### 3. **Frontend**
- Responding at `http://localhost:3000`

### 4. **Docker**
- Docker and Docker Compose are installed
- Port conflict: Docker DB was using 5432 (already used by local PostgreSQL). Host port changed to **5433** in `docker/docker-compose.yml` so both can run

---

## Changes made

### Dependencies
- **Next.js**: `14.2.18` → `14.2.35` (security fixes)
- **eslint-config-next**: `14.2.18` → `14.2.35`
- **uvicorn**: added `<1.0` upper bound for compatibility

### Verification script
- Added `./scripts/verify.sh` to check:
  - Docker availability
  - Database connection
  - Tables and row counts
  - Backend health
  - Frontend availability

### Docker
- DB host port changed from `5432` to `5433` to avoid conflict with local PostgreSQL
- README updated with troubleshooting for this port conflict

---

## How to run verification

```bash
./scripts/verify.sh
```

## Docker usage

```bash
docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

The DB container listens on host port **5433** (internal port 5432). Backend and frontend containers connect to the DB over the internal Docker network.

**Note:** `start-dev.sh` uses local PostgreSQL on port 5432. Docker Compose uses its own DB on port 5433, so both setups can run at the same time.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Checking fetch errors**

I'm investigating the 'failed to fetch' error by examining potential CORS, network, and URL configuration issues. The fetch error likely stems from CORS policy blocking requests from localhost:3000 to localhost:8000 despite configured CORS, or the backend service may not be running. The 'failed to fetch' error likely occurs because the API endpoint returns a 204 No Content response, causing `res.json()` to throw an error in the `api()` function. The 'failed to fetch' error likely stems from network issues or CORS problems, not from the API returning a 204 status. 

**Checking auth requirements**

The cameras list endpoint likely requires AdminUser, while startStream only needs CurrentUser. The API_URL is hardcoded to localhost:8000, bypassing Next.js rewrites that proxy /api to the backend. I need to verify NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in production and ensure the backend is running at the expected port. I'm updating the API URL construction to use relative paths instead of hardcoded localhost:8000. I'm implementing a fallback to use `window.location.origin` for fetch when `API_URL` is not set, to handle CORS issues with backend rewrites. </details></think>

Here’s what was changed:

## 1. **"Failed to fetch" when starting stream**

API calls were going directly to `http://localhost:8000` from the frontend on `http://localhost:3000`, which can cause CORS or connection issues.

**Change:** Use the same origin when `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is not set so requests go through the Next.js rewrite proxy:

- Added `getApiBase()` in `api.ts`:
  - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use it
  - Otherwise in the browser → use `window.location.origin` (same-origin)
  - On the server → use `http://localhost:8000`
- Updated `api()`, stream URL, and login to use `getApiBase()`.

Requests now go to `http://localhost:3000/api/...`, and Next.js proxies them to the backend, avoiding CORS and connection errors.

---

## 2. **Dashboard updates (Minimals-style)**

The dashboard now includes:

- **Stat cards** – Total Users, Total Cameras, Today’s Detections, Unknown Today (clickable, link to related pages)
- **Detection trends chart** – 7-day line chart
- **Recent detections** – Last 5 detections with status (known/unknown) and confidence
- **Recent alerts** – Last 5 alerts with type and severity

Layout and styling:

- Card hover effects
- Clear section headers
- “View all” links to Detections and Alerts
- Responsive grid layout

---

## 3. **New backend endpoints**

- `GET /api/v1/analytics/recent-detections?limit=5`
- `GET /api/v1/analytics/recent-alerts?limit=5`

---

## If "failed to fetch" still occurs

1. **Backend running** – Ensure the backend is running on port 8000 (`./scripts/start-dev.sh backend` or `./scripts/start-dev.sh`).
2. **Next.js rewrite** – Ensure `next.config.js` has the rewrite rule for `/api/`.
3. **Production** – Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` to your backend URL (e.g. `https://api.yourdomain.com`).

---


Here’s what was done:

---

## 1. **Python files in a React project**

The `.py` files are correct and should stay as they are.

Visioryx is a full-stack app:

- **Backend (Python/FastAPI)** – API, AI, database, auth
- **Frontend (React/Next.js)** – UI and dashboard

They talk over HTTP. The frontend calls the backend API; the backend does not need to be rewritten in JavaScript.

An **Architecture** section was added to the README to document this.

---

## 2. **Dashboard UI updates**

The dashboard layout and styling were updated:

### Stat cards
- Larger icons with colored backgrounds
- “View details” link with arrow
- Hover effects (lift + shadow)
- Decorative circle in the corner
- Full-width on mobile, 4 columns on desktop

### Chart
- Area + line chart with gradient fill
- Clear section header with icon
- Adjusted spacing and typography

### Sections
- Clear “Overview” header and subtitle
- Section headers with icons
- “View all” links for detections and alerts
- Improved empty states

### Theme
- Softer card shadows and borders
- More consistent spacing

---

## 3. **Optional: other UI libraries**

If you want a different look, you could add:

- **shadcn/ui** – Tailwind-based components
- **Chakra UI** – Component library
- **DaisyUI** – Tailwind component classes

Right now the dashboard uses **MUI (Material UI)** with custom styling. If you want to switch to another library, say which one and we can plan the migration.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Adding illustration assets**

I'm researching free illustration sources like undraw.co and popsy.co for the surveillance dashboard UI. I'm adding illustration assets for 'no detections' and 'no alerts' states using free sources like Popsy and SVG placeholders. 

I'm creating inline SVG illustrations for empty state detections and alerts using Popsy sources to avoid external dependencies. I'm creating simple, professional SVG illustrations for dashboard overview, empty detections state, and empty alerts state in the public folder. I'm using illustrations from Illustrations.popsy.co and adding them as SVGs to the assets/illustrations folder. 

I'm adding SVG illustrations to the public/assets/illustrations folder for use in the frontend. I'm using Popsy illustrations for the dashboard empty state, with URLs like https://illustrations.popsy.co/amber/security.svg. 

I'm replacing Popsy illustrations with Undraw API images for better professionalism and consistency. </details></think>

[Minimal - MUI Store](https://mui.com/store/contributors/minimal)<br/><br/># Minimals.cc Dashboard Illustration Images & MUI<br/><br/>**Minimals.cc** is a professional React dashboard UI kit built with Material-UI (MUI) components.[1] It comes in both free and premium versions.<br/><br/>## Free Version<br/>The **Minimal Free** dashboard is available for free and includes 6 pages with basic theme customization.[3] It's built with React and Material UI components, featuring pages for dashboard, users, products, posts, and sign-in.[3]<br/><br/>## Premium Version<br/>The **Minimal - Client and Admin Dashboard** (paid version) offers significantly more features, including:[2]<br/>- 70+ example pages<br/>- Multiple framework options: Next.js and Vite.js (both JavaScript and TypeScript)<br/>- Design Figma file for extending development<br/>- Complete theme customization<br/>- Authentication options (JWT, Firebase, Amplify, Auth0, Supabase)<br/>- Light/dark mode support<br/>- 1 year of free updates and 6 months of technical support<br/><br/>## Image Resources<br/>The dashboard includes professional illustrations and visual assets integrated throughout its interface. The template comes with ready-to-use Material-UI components and includes a complete Figma design file (in Plus and Extended packages) to customize illustrations and design elements.[1][2]<br/><br/>Both versions are available on the [MUI Store](https://mui.com/store/) and can be previewed live before purchase or download. |

[feat(next/image): support `new URL()` for `images.remotePatterns` · Pull Request #77692 · vercel/next.js](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/77692)<br/><br/># Next.js 14 remotePatterns Configuration<br/><br/>## Overview<br/>`remotePatterns` is a configuration option in `next.config.js` that allows you to safely specify which external image URLs can be optimized by Next.js. This prevents malicious actors from optimizing unintended URLs.[1][3]<br/><br/>## Configuration Methods<br/><br/>### Object Syntax (Standard)<br/>```js<br/>module.exports = \{<br/>  images: \{<br/>    remotePatterns: [<br/>      \{<br/>        protocol: 'https',<br/>        hostname: 's3.amazonaws.com',<br/>        port: '',<br/>        pathname: '/my-bucket/**',<br/>        search: '',<br/>      \},<br/>    ],<br/>  \},<br/>\}<br/>```[3]<br/><br/>### URL Syntax (Version 15.3.0+)<br/>```js<br/>module.exports = \{<br/>  images: \{<br/>    remotePatterns: [<br/>      new URL('https://s3.amazonaws.com/my-bucket/**'),<br/>    ],<br/>  \},<br/>\}<br/>```[1][5]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>**Wildcard Patterns:**<br/>- `**` matches any number of path segments or subdomains (only at the end or beginning)[1]<br/>- `*` matches a single path segment or subdomain[1]<br/><br/>Example allowing subdomains:<br/>```js<br/>\{<br/>  protocol: 'https',<br/>  hostname: '**.example.com',<br/>\}<br/>```[1]<br/><br/>**Query String Control:**<br/>You can restrict query strings using the `search` property. If omitted, all query strings are blocked by default (unless specified).[1]<br/><br/>**Security Note:** When omitting protocol, port, pathname, or search, wildcards (`**`) are implied, which is not recommended as it may allow malicious optimization.[1] |

</details>

---


Here’s what was added to bring the app closer to minimals.cc-style visuals while keeping MUI only:

## Illustration components

1. **`EmptyStateIllustration`** – Document/magnifying glass SVG for empty states  
2. **`ChartEmptyIllustration`** – Chart/analytics empty state  
3. **`WelcomeIllustration`** – Dashboard hero with monitor, chart bars, camera, and shield icons  

## Where they’re used

| Page        | Usage                                                                 |
|-------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Dashboard** | Welcome illustration in the header (lg+), empty states for detections, alerts, and chart |
| **Analytics** | Chart empty state when there’s no data                                |
| **Alerts**    | Empty state when there are no alerts                                 |
| **Detections** | Empty state when there are no detections                             |

## Reusable components

- **`EmptyState`** – Combines illustration + message for consistent empty states  
- **`next.config.js`** – `images.remotePatterns` for Unsplash and Pexels so you can use their URLs with `next/image` later  

## Asset sources (per minimals.cc)

- **Images**: Pexels, Unsplash, Freepik  
- **Icons**: Iconify, Flaticon, Freepik  

To add photos (e.g. hero or user avatars), place them in `frontend/public/assets/` or use Unsplash/Pexels URLs with the Next.js `Image` component. The SVG illustrations are self-contained and don’t require external assets.

---


Here’s what those minimals.cc resources offer and how they relate to Visioryx:

## Minimals.cc resources

**[docs.minimals.cc](https://docs.minimals.cc/)** – Main docs for the Minimal UI Kit (theme, layout, routing, auth, etc.).

**[minimals.cc/components/foundation/icons](https://minimals.cc/components/foundation/icons)** – Icon setup:

- **Material Icons** – [MUI Icons](https://mui.com/components/icons/#main-content)
- **Iconify** – [iconify.design/icon-sets](https://iconify.design/icon-sets)
- Local, social, file, flag, asset icons, and illustrations

## How this maps to Visioryx

1. **Icons** – Visioryx already uses MUI Material Icons. You can add **Iconify** for more variety (e.g. via `@iconify/react`).
2. **Illustrations** – Minimals uses asset illustrations (e.g. the welcome card). Visioryx has custom SVG illustrations; you can add more from Pexels/Unsplash or similar.
3. **Layout** – Your dashboard structure (sidebar, header, stat cards, charts) is already similar to minimals.

## Possible next steps

| Area | Option |
|------|--------|
| **Icons** | Add Iconify for extra icons (e.g. `@iconify/react`) |
| **Welcome card** | Add a larger hero card with illustration and CTA (like minimals) |
| **Featured card** | Add a “Featured” or “Quick actions” card with background image |
| **Stat cards** | Add trend indicators (e.g. +2.6%, -0.1%) where you have metrics |
| **Assets** | Use [docs.minimals.cc/credit-assets](https://docs.minimals.cc/credit-assets) for Pexels, Unsplash, Freepik, etc. |

If you say which of these you want (e.g. Iconify, welcome card, featured card, or stat trends), I can outline concrete implementation steps for Visioryx.

---


Here’s what was implemented:

## Changes Implemented

### 1. **Welcome card** (minimals-style)
- Dark gradient card with greeting: `Hi, {name} 👋` (name from email)
- Short description of the surveillance system
- “Go to Live” CTA button
- Illustration on the right (hidden on small screens)

### 2. **Featured card**
- Gradient card (blue → primary) with “Quick Access”
- Links to Live Monitoring
- Abstract gradient overlays
- Hover lift effect

### 3. **Stat cards with trend**
- “Today’s Detections” shows **7-day trend** (e.g. `+2.6% vs 7d` or `-0.1% vs 7d`)
- Green for positive, red for negative
- Trend computed in backend: this week vs previous week

### 4. **Backend**
- `GET /api/v1/analytics/overview` now returns `detection_trend_7d`
- Compares detection count for last 7 days vs previous 7 days

### 5. **Chart empty state**
- Chart area only renders when there is data
- Empty state with illustration when there is no data

### New components
- `frontend/src/components/dashboard/WelcomeCard.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/dashboard/FeaturedCard.tsx`
- `frontend/src/components/dashboard/index.ts`

### Layout
- Top row: Welcome card (8 cols) + Featured card (4 cols)
- Second row: 4 stat cards
- Third row: Detection trends chart + Recent detections
- Fourth row: Recent alerts

Restart the dev server to see the changes. If you want Iconify icons added later, say which screens or components to update.

```json
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "@app.websocket(\"/ws\")",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "async def websocket_endpoint(websocket: WebSocket):",
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"WebSocket for real-time events.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "    import uuid",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "    client_id = str(uuid.uuid4())",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "    await ws_manager.connect(websocket, client_id)",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    try:",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "        while True:",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "            data = await websocket.receive_text()",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "            # Heartbeat / keepalive",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "            if data == \"ping\":",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "                await websocket.send_text(\"pong\")",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "    except Exception:",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "        pass",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "    finally:",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "        ws_manager.disconnect(client_id)",
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.4618696868419647
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/services/stream_manager.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 53,
            "column": 25
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 119
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "# Global: camera_id -&gt; latest JPEG bytes",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "_frame_buffer: dict[int, bytes] = {}",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "_frame_lock = threading.Lock()",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "_active_cameras: dict[int, dict] = {}  # camera_id -&gt; {rtsp_url, thread, stop_event}",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "def _capture_loop(camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str, stop_event: threading.Event):",
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Background thread: capture frames and update buffer.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Test/demo mode - no real RTSP",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "    if rtsp_url.startswith(\"test://\") or rtsp_url.startswith(\"demo://\"):",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "        try:",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "            while not stop_event.is_set():",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "                frame_bytes = _generate_test_frame(camera_id)",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "                with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "                    _frame_buffer[camera_id] = frame_bytes",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "                stop_event.wait(0.1)",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "        except Exception as e:",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "            logger.error(f\"Camera {camera_id} test mode error: {e}\")",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "        finally:",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "            with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "                _frame_buffer.pop(camera_id, None)",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "        return",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "    cap = cv2.VideoCapture(rtsp_url, cv2.CAP_FFMPEG)",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "    if not cap.isOpened():",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "        logger.error(f\"Camera {camera_id}: failed to open {rtsp_url}\")",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "        # Put placeholder so MJPEG has something to show",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "        with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "            _frame_buffer[camera_id] = _get_no_signal_frame()",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "        return",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "    settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "    skip = settings.FRAME_SKIP_RATE",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "    count = 0",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "    det_count = 0",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "    try:",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "        while not stop_event.is_set():",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "            ret, frame = cap.read()",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "            if not ret or frame is None:",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "                break",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "            count += 1",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "            if count % (skip + 1) == 0:",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "                det_count += 1",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "                frame = annotate_frame(frame, det_count, camera_id=camera_id, run_detection_every=3)",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "                _, jpeg = cv2.imencode(\".jpg\", frame)",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "                with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "                    _frame_buffer[camera_id] = jpeg.tobytes()",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    except Exception as e:",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "        logger.error(f\"Camera {camera_id} capture error: {e}\")",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "    finally:",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "        cap.release()",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "        with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "            _frame_buffer.pop(camera_id, None)",
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "def start_stream(camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str) -&gt; bool:",
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Start capturing from camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 112
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.45054519176483154
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/services/stream_manager.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 53
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Camera Stream Manager",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Manages per-camera frame capture and MJPEG streaming.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "Supports test:// URLs for demo when no real cameras available.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "import os",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "import threading",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "import time",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "import cv2",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "import numpy as np",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.config import get_settings",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.logger import get_logger",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.services.detection_overlay import annotate_frame",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = get_logger(\"stream_manager\")",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "# Use TCP for RTSP (more reliable than UDP through firewalls/NAT)",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "os.environ.setdefault(\"OPENCV_FFMPEG_CAPTURE_OPTIONS\", \"rtsp_transport;tcp\")",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "# Placeholder \"No signal\" frame (gray 640x480 with text)",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "_NO_SIGNAL_FRAME: Optional[bytes] = None",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "def _get_no_signal_frame() -&gt; bytes:",
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Generate a placeholder frame for when no camera signal.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    global _NO_SIGNAL_FRAME",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    if _NO_SIGNAL_FRAME is not None:",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        return _NO_SIGNAL_FRAME",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    img = np.zeros((480, 640, 3), dtype=np.uint8)",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    img[:] = (40, 40, 40)",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    cv2.putText(img, \"No signal\", (220, 220), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 1.5, (255, 255, 255), 2)",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    cv2.putText(img, \"Check RTSP URL or use test:// for demo\", (100, 270), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.6, (180, 180, 180), 1)",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "    _, jpeg = cv2.imencode(\".jpg\", img)",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    _NO_SIGNAL_FRAME = jpeg.tobytes()",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    return _NO_SIGNAL_FRAME",
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "def _generate_test_frame(camera_id: int) -&gt; Optional[bytes]:",
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Generate a test pattern for test:// URLs.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 42
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.41986316442489624
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/services/stream_manager.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 37,
            "column": 27
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 57,
            "column": 36
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def _generate_test_frame(camera_id: int) -&gt; Optional[bytes]:",
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Generate a test pattern for test:// URLs.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    t = time.time()",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    x = np.linspace(0, 1, 640)",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "    y = np.linspace(0, 1, 480)",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "    xx, yy = np.meshgrid(x, y)",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    r = ((xx + t * 0.1) % 1 * 255).astype(np.uint8)",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "    g = ((yy + t * 0.05) % 1 * 255).astype(np.uint8)",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    b = np.full_like(r, 128)",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "    img = np.stack([b, g, r], axis=2)  # BGR for OpenCV",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    cv2.putText(img, f\"Camera {camera_id} - Test\", (180, 220), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 1, (255, 255, 255), 2)",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    cv2.putText(img, \"Demo stream (use real RTSP for live feed)\", (100, 270), cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX, 0.6, (200, 200, 200), 1)",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "    _, jpeg = cv2.imencode(\".jpg\", img)",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "    return jpeg.tobytes()",
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "# Global: camera_id -&gt; latest JPEG bytes",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "_frame_buffer: dict[int, bytes] = {}",
            "lineNumber": 58
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.33061444759368896
    },

          {
            "text": "@router.post(\"/{camera_id}/start\")",

          },
          {
            "text": "async def start_camera_stream(",
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    camera_id: int,",
            "lineNumber": 73,

          },
          {

          },
          {

            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "):",
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Start camera stream (capture begins).\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "    result = await db.execute(select(Camera).where(Camera.id == camera_id))",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "    camera = result.scalar_one_or_none()",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "    if not camera:",

          },
          {
            "text": "        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Camera not found\")",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "    start_stream(camera_id, camera.rtsp_url)",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "    camera.status = \"active\"",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "    await db.commit()",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "    return {\"status\": \"started\", \"camera_id\": camera_id}",
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "@router.post(\"/{camera_id}/stop\")",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "async def stop_camera_stream(",

          },
          {
            "text": "    camera_id: int,",

          },
          {
            "text": "    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),",

          },
          {

            "text": "    ",

        ]

          {

          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def start_stream(camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str) -&gt; bool:",
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Start capturing from camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "    if camera_id in _active_cameras:",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "        return True",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "    stop_event = threading.Event()",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "    t = threading.Thread(target=_capture_loop, args=(camera_id, rtsp_url, stop_event), daemon=True)",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "    t.start()",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "    _active_cameras[camera_id] = {\"rtsp_url\": rtsp_url, \"thread\": t, \"stop_event\": stop_event}",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "    logger.info(f\"Stream started for camera {camera_id}\")",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "    return True",
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "def stop_stream(camera_id: int):",
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Stop capturing from camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 124
          }
        ]

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/services/stream_manager.py",

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def get_frame(camera_id: int) -&gt; Optional[bytes]:",
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Get latest JPEG frame for camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "    with _frame_lock:",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "text": "        return _frame_buffer.get(camera_id)",
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "def is_streaming(camera_id: int) -&gt; bool:",
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Check if camera is actively streaming.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 142
          }
        ]

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/services/camera_worker.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 71
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Camera Worker",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Per-camera frame capture and processing.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "import asyncio",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "import cv2",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "import numpy as np",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.config import get_settings",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.logger import get_logger",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "from app.core.websocket_manager import ws_manager",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = get_logger(\"camera_worker\")",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "class CameraWorker:",
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Process frames from a single camera.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    def __init__(self, camera_id: int, rtsp_url: str, frame_callback=None):",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "        self.camera_id = camera_id",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "        self.rtsp_url = rtsp_url",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "        self.frame_callback = frame_callback",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._running = False",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._cap: Optional[cv2.VideoCapture] = None",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._frame_count = 0",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    async def start(self):",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Start capturing frames.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._running = True",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "        settings = get_settings()",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "        skip = settings.FRAME_SKIP_RATE",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._cap = cv2.VideoCapture(self.rtsp_url)",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "        if not self._cap.isOpened():",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "            logger.error(f\"Camera {self.camera_id}: failed to open {self.rtsp_url}\")",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "            await ws_manager.broadcast(\"camera_status\", {",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"camera_id\": self.camera_id,",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"status\": \"error\",",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "                \"message\": \"Failed to open stream\",",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "            })",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "            return",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "        logger.info(f\"Camera {self.camera_id}: started\")",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "        await ws_manager.broadcast(\"camera_status\", {",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"camera_id\": self.camera_id,",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"status\": \"active\",",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "        })",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "        try:",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "            while self._running:",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "                ret, frame = await loop.run_in_executor(None, lambda: self._cap.read())",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "                if not ret or frame is None:",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "                    break",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "                self._frame_count += 1",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "                if self._frame_count % (skip + 1) == 0 and self.frame_callback:",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "                    await self.frame_callback(self.camera_id, frame)",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "        except asyncio.CancelledError:",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "            pass",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "        finally:",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "            self.stop()",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "    def stop(self):",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "        \"\"\"Stop capture.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "        self._running = False",
            "lineNumber": 64
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.2874964475631714
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./backend/app/core/config.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 66,

          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "Visioryx - Core Configuration",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "Centralized configuration management using Pydantic Settings.",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "from functools import lru_cache",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "class Settings(BaseSettings):",
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Application settings loaded from environment variables.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "        env_file=\".env\",",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "        env_file_encoding=\"utf-8\",",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "        case_sensitive=False,",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Application",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    APP_NAME: str = \"Visioryx\"",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    APP_VERSION: str = \"1.0.0\"",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    DEBUG: bool = False",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    # API",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    API_V1_PREFIX: str = \"/api/v1\"",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Database",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    DATABASE_URL: str = \"postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx\"",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    DATABASE_URL_SYNC: str = \"postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/visioryx\"",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "    # JWT Security",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    SECRET_KEY: str = \"change-this-in-production-use-openssl-rand-hex-32\"",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    ALGORITHM: str = \"HS256\"",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES: int = 60 * 24  # 24 hours",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Face Recognition",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "    FACE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD: float = 0.6  # Cosine similarity threshold",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    FACE_DETECTION_CONFIDENCE: float = 0.5",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    EMBEDDING_DIMENSION: int = 512",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Object Detection",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    OBJECT_DETECTION_CONFIDENCE: float = 0.5",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    OBJECT_DETECTION_IOU_THRESHOLD: float = 0.45",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Processing",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    FRAME_SKIP_RATE: int = 2  # Process every Nth frame",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "    MAX_CAMERAS: int = 16",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    FRAME_QUEUE_SIZE: int = 10",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Storage Paths",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    STORAGE_PATH: str = \"storage\"",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "    REGISTERED_FACES_PATH: str = \"storage/registered_faces\"",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "    UNKNOWN_FACES_PATH: str = \"storage/unknown_faces\"",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "    SNAPSHOTS_PATH: str = \"storage/snapshots\"",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "    # WebSocket",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "    WS_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL: int = 30",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    # GPU (Optional)",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "    CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES: Optional[str] = None",
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "@lru_cache",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "def get_settings() -&gt; Settings:",
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Cached settings instance.\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "    return Settings()",
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
```
